0.description: Ordered list of cdi:InstanceVariables (inline cdifInstanceVariable or @id-reference). Position is implicit in array order.
The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.
Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).
The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).
The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).
reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset
Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).

The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.
The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).
The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.
True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.
The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.

CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.
CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.
The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).
Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).
Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).
Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).
Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.
The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).
Per-category Statistic value objects.
Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.
CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.
identifier or name for the data type concept.

Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.

Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.
maximum numeric value in the dataset
Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.
Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
text label for the term that is useful to human user
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation
minimum numeric value in the dataset
string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization
identifier or name for the property concept
URI or code identifying the unit of measure
unit of measurement as text
Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).
URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).

properties.description: Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.
A cdi:DescriptorValueDomain enumerating the codes that can appear in the descriptor column, each paired (via cdif:isDefinedBy) with the RepresentedVariable that the code names.
Ordered list of descriptor entries. Each entry has a cdif:value (the code as it appears in the descriptor column) and a cdif:isDefinedBy pointer to the RepresentedVariable that the code names.
The code value as it appears in the descriptor column.
Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.
Qualifies the purpose or use expressed as a paired external controlled vocabulary.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.

The unit in which the data values are measured (kg, pound, euro), expressed as a value from a controlled system of entries (i.e., QDT). Supports the provision of an identifier for the entry in the authoritative source (a URI, etc.), and the specific vocabulary.
Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
Classification code for this concept within a scheme.
Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.
Note clarifying meaning and/or intended use.
General note about this concept.
Note about past state or use.
Note documenting a change.
Note for editors and maintainers.
Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.
Concept scheme(s) for which this is a top concept.
Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
The data type intended to be used by this variable. Supports the optional use of an external controlled vocabulary.
Sentinel (missing / not-applicable) value domain(s) for this RepresentedVariable (RepresentedVariable.takesSentinelValuesFrom).
The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.
Identifier for this enumerated (categorical) domain.
Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic).
CDIF codelist (skos:ConceptScheme) whose concepts define the allowed values of this enumeration domain.
Top-level concepts that have no skos:broader in this scheme. Required for hierarchical codelists.
Broader (parent) concepts. Required on any concept that appears as a skos:narrower value of another concept. CDIF requires both directions to be explicit for hierarchy traversal.
Narrower (child) concepts. If present, each inline concept must also declare skos:broader pointing back to the parent concept. Both skos:narrower and skos:broader must be explicit in CDIF codelists.
Formal definition of this concept. Optional for CDIF codelist concepts. A plain string.
Classification code for this concept within a scheme.
Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
The concept scheme this concept belongs to. Required for CDIF codelist concepts.
General note about the concept scheme.
Formal explanation of the meaning or purpose of this concept scheme.
Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
Text statement of access conditions for the codelist.
License for the codelist.
Web location of a page describing the codelist.
ISO 8601 date when the codelist was last modified.
Primary identifier for the codelist. May be a string URI or a structured PropertyValue.
Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.
The substantive value domain for this RepresentedVariable - the set of valid, meaningful values (RepresentedVariable.takesSubstantiveValuesFrom).
The data types that are recommended for use with this domain.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

The unit in which the data values are measured (kg, pound, euro), expressed as a simple string, in cases where no additional information is available (in the legacy system) or needed (as in the case of broad agreement within the community of use [i.e., ISO country codes, currencies, etc. in SDMX])
A short natural language account of the characteristics of the object.

If value is False, the members are unique within the collection - if True, there may be duplicates. (Note that a mathematical “bag” permits duplicates and is unordered - a “set” does not have duplicates and may be ordered.)

A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.
URL of the referenced resource.
Human-readable name of the referenced resource.
Human-readable description of the reference.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Human understandable name (liguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.
Intent or reason for the object/the description of the object.
Indicates the type of relationship, nominal, ordinal, interval, ratio, or continuous. Use where appropriate for the representation type.
A formal description of the set of values in human-readable language.

A pattern for a number as described in Unicode Locale Data Markup Language (LDML) (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35.html) Part 3: Numbers (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35-numbers.html#Number_Format_Patterns) and Part 4. Dates (http://www.unicode.org/reports/tr35/tr35-dates.html#Date_Format_Patterns) . Examples would be #,##0.### to describe the pattern for a decimal number, or yyyy.MM.dd G 'at' HH:mm:ss zzz for a datetime pattern.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

A logical expression where the values of "x" making the expression true are the members of the set of valid values. For example, "(all reals x such that x > 0)" describes the real numbers greater than 0.

A string denoting the maximum possible value (excluding this value). From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "maxExclusive: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the maximum valid value (exclusive). The value of this property becomes the maximum exclusive annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

A string denoting the maximum possible value. From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "maximum: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the maximum valid value (inclusive); equivalent to maxInclusive. The value of this property becomes the maximum annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

A string denoting the minimum possible value (excluding this value). From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "minExclusive: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the minimum valid value (exclusive). The value of this property becomes the minimum exclusive annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."

A string denoting the minimum possible value. From the W3C Recommendation "Metadata Vocabulary for Tabular Data" (https://www.w3.org/TR/tabular-metadata/) 5.11.2: "minimum: An atomic property that contains a single number or string that is the minimum valid value (inclusive); equivalent to minInclusive. The value of this property becomes the minimum annotation for the described datatype. See Value Constraints in [tabular-data-model] for details."
A regular expression where strings matching the expression belong to the set of valid values. Use typeOfContent to specify the syntax of the regularExpression found in content.
Content of the property expressed as a simple string.
Optional use of a controlled vocabulary to specifically type the associated content.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.
Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

Natural language statement conveying the meaning of a concept, differentiating it from other concepts. Supports the use of multiple languages and structured text. 'externalDefinition' can't be used if 'definition' is used.
A short natural language account of the characteristics of the object.

Kind of unit of measure, so that it may be prone to translation to equivalent UOMs. Example values include "acceleration," "temperature," "salinity", etc. This description exists at the conceptual level, indicating a limitation on the type of representations which may be used for the variable as it is made more concrete.

Natural language statement conveying the meaning of a concept, differentiating it from other concepts. Supports the use of multiple languages and structured text. 'externalDefinition' can't be used if 'definition' is used.
A human-readable display label for the object. Supports the use of multiple languages. Repeat for labels with different content, for example, labels with differing length limitations.

A reference to an external definition of a concept (that is, a concept which is described outside the content of the DDI-CDI metadata description). An example is a SKOS concept. The definition property is assumed to duplicate the external one referenced if externalDefinition is used. Other corresponding properties are assumed to be included unchanged if used.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Human understandable name (linguistic signifier, word, phrase, or mnemonic). May follow ISO/IEC 11179-5 naming principles, and have context provided to specify usage.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
A LongDataStructure must include exactly one IdentifierComponent, exactly one VariableDescriptorComponent, exactly one VariableValueComponent, plus zero or more AttributeComponents.
